entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: A climate-resilient and highly nutritious small grain crop grown in semi-arid tropics.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographic region where finger millet is widely cultivated.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The productivity of the crop, currently characterized by a significant gap between mean and potential yield.

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Biological factors that act as production constraints for finger millet.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Non-biological environmental factors that act as production constraints for finger millet.

entities.name: GENETIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Valuable genetic materials found in centers of diversity and global gene banks used for variety design.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The diversity within the species that can be harnessed to integrate essential traits into varieties.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING METHODS
entities.description: Traditional breeding approaches used to integrate traits into candidate varieties.

entities.name: MODERN BREEDING METHODS
entities.description: Advanced breeding approaches used to integrate traits into candidate varieties.

entities.name: GENOMICS-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding innovation used to accelerate variety design and deployment.

entities.name: MUTATION BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding innovation used to accelerate variety design and deployment.

entities.name: GENOME EDITING
entities.description: A genetic innovation used to accelerate variety design and deployment.

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL CONTENTS
entities.description: The nutritional value and components of finger millet grains.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC QUALITY TRAITS
entities.description: Traits related to the growth and production performance of the crop.

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL QUALITY TRAITS
entities.description: Traits related to the nutritional quality of the crop.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of the crop to withstand water-limited conditions.

entities.name: SALINITY TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of the crop to withstand high soil salinity.

entities.name: FUNGAL DISEASES
entities.description: Pathogenic fungal infections that affect finger millet.

entities.name: WEEDS
entities.description: Unwanted plants that compete with finger millet for resources.

entities.name: INSECT PESTS
entities.description: Insects that cause damage to finger millet crops.

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: Genomic data and tools available for the improvement of finger millet.

entities.name: REFERENCE GENOMES
entities.description: Standardized genome sequences used as a baseline for genetic analysis.

entities.name: WHOLE GENOME RE-SEQUENCING
entities.description: A technology used to analyze genetic variation across the entire genome of the crop.

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTOMICS
entities.description: The study of the transcriptome to understand gene expression in finger millet.

entities.name: GENETIC ENGINEERING
entities.description: The direct manipulation of an organism's genes to achieve desired traits.

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The trait of reaching maturity in a shorter timeframe.

entities.name: SEED COLOUR
entities.description: The physical color of the seeds, a desirable product profile trait.

entities.name: COMPACT HEAD TYPE
entities.description: The physical structure of the grain head, a desirable product profile trait.

entities.name: FOOD AND FEED NUTRIENTS QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of nutrients for both human food and animal feed.

entities.name: MARKETABILITY
entities.description: The quality of the crop that makes it desirable for commercial sale.
